ApplyTranslations.description:
Indicates if the translation (custom names) should be applied to data being returned
.. default = true

ApplyVisibility.description:
Indicates if site / product / user / role visibility conditions should be applied
.. default = true

IsCrossSiteRequest.description:
Indicates if the requests should span across all sites
.. only possible if flag provided and on GlobalAdminSite
.. default = false

IsTrainingRequest.description: Indicates if this request was specifically tagged as a training request

WrapRequestInWriteAndWaitContext.description:
Indicates if the entire api request should be wrapped in a using (Data.Context.WriteAndWaitForReplication())
.. default = false

RequestOptionFlags.description:
0 = None
1 = IncludeDeleted
2 = FlushChanges
4 = IsTraning
8 = DontTranslate
16 = SkipVisibilityCheck
32 = SkipProductCheck

info.description: Explore the API endpoints within this section to access and interact with slas.  Each endpoint has its own requirements and input / output definitions so be sure to review the parameters and data types in detail.
info.title: SLAs

get.description:
#### Retrieves a list of metrics. A specific metric can be retrieved via GET `api/v1.0/metrics/{MetricId}`.
#### Sample request:
```
GET /api/v1.0/metrics/
```

parameters.description: Request Options specified for the Metric(s)
parameters.name: r

parameters.description: Locate your API token under Administration > Developer tools and place after the text "Bearer " without quotes
parameters.name: Authorization

parameters.description: Recommended content-type for use with the Incident IQ API ( JSON )
parameters.name: Content-Type

get.description:
#### Retrieves a list of metrics for a specific SLA.
#### Sample request:
```
GET /api/v1.0/metrics/for/sla/bd64e104-4c83-4744-a888-eeb760c03bfe
```

parameters.description: SlaId of SLA containing the Metrics being requested
parameters.name: SlaId

parameters.description: Request Options specified for the Sla Metrics
parameters.name: r

parameters.description: Locate your API token under Administration > Developer tools and place after the text "Bearer " without quotes
parameters.name: Authorization

parameters.description: Recommended content-type for use with the Incident IQ API ( JSON )
parameters.name: Content-Type

get.description:
#### Retrieves a list of metric types. A specific metric type can be retrieved via GET `api/v1.0/metrics/types/{MetricTypeId}`.
#### Sample request:
```
GET /api/v1.0/metrics/types
```

parameters.description: Request Options specified for the MetricType(s)
parameters.name: r

parameters.description: Locate your API token under Administration > Developer tools and place after the text "Bearer " without quotes
parameters.name: Authorization

parameters.description: Recommended content-type for use with the Incident IQ API ( JSON )
parameters.name: Content-Type

delete.description:
#### Delete a specific SLA Metric Type
#### Sample request:
```
DELETE /api/v1.0/metrics/types/67a39334-d778-487c-95ae-07a776ed8201
```

parameters.description: MetricTypeID of the MetricType to be deleted
parameters.name: MetricTypeId

parameters.description: Locate your API token under Administration > Developer tools and place after the text "Bearer " without quotes
parameters.name: Authorization

parameters.description: Recommended content-type for use with the Incident IQ API ( JSON )
parameters.name: Content-Type
